assertions.title: Editing a stopped entry updates the canonical fact
assertions.reason: Assigned browser surface could not be exercised because the required frontend runtime on http://127.0.0.1:5173 was unavailable (connection refused).

steps.observed: curl failed with connection refused on port 5173
Backend responded HTTP 404 with JSON body {"message":"no matching operation was found"}, proving port reachability but not a documented health endpoint

steps.expected: Timer UI loads for stopped-entry editor validation

evidence.consoleErrors: blocked: browser page could not be opened because the frontend runtime was unavailable
evidence.network: curl http://127.0.0.1:5173 -> connection refused; curl http://127.0.0.1:8080/health -> HTTP 404 {"message":"no matching operation was found"}; agent-browser open http://127.0.0.1:5173 -> net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED

assertions.issues: Runtime readiness failure on the assigned frontend port prevented execution of the user-visible stopped-entry edit flow and the required reopen/readback proof.

blockers.description: Frontend runtime on the assigned browser port 5173 was unavailable throughout validation attempts.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried with direct curl readiness check and agent-browser navigation; both returned connection refused.

summary: VAL-ENTRY-002 blocked: backend port 8080 was reachable, but the required frontend port 5173 was not accepting connections, so the stopped-entry editor flow could not be validated through the browser surface.
